<!-- GENERATED by scripts/build-codex.mjs from plugins/relay/skills/review/ ‚Äî do not hand-edit; re-run the generator. -->

# review ‚Äî respond to a thought (ack / comment / change)

Answer a thought pointing at you. A review is **the same shape as a `report`** ‚Äî an append-only thought ‚Äî that **references the thought it answers** (by id). Your response *is* the resolution; an agreeing review is what `relay-settle` later pins as a decision. No @-set, no graduation ceremony ‚Äî that's the point of a 1‚Äì2 person relay.

## Scope

Operates on the **content repo** ‚Äî a *separate* coordination repo located via `$RELAY_REPO`, else a cached prior resolution, else the current dir if it has `relay.yml`, else **ask the user** and cache the answer (never assume cwd; see AGENTS.md). Writes **one append-only thought** (`thoughts/<date>-<handle>-<slug>.md`); shows a diff and is gated.

## Process

### Step 1 ‚Äî Resolve + pull + find what's @me
- **Resolve who's running** (git author email ‚Üí `git:` in `relay.yml` ‚Üí your handle), **pull**, then find thoughts addressed to you (`@<your-handle>`) you haven't answered ‚Äî `relay-digest` lists them.

### Step 2 ‚Äî Respond by id
Write `thoughts/<date>-<handle>-<slug>.md`:
```markdown
---
date: <ISO>
by: <handle>
subject: "re: <what you're answering>"     # quote it ‚Äî the "re:" colon breaks unquoted YAML
thread: "<link to the discussion's opening thought>"   # REQUIRED, quote it (a markdown link starts with [ = a YAML sequence) ‚Äî the grouping anchor (the root); digest groups by it
re: "<link to the exact thought you answer>"           # quote it ‚Äî the immediate parent; omit when it == thread (a direct reply to the root)
relate:                                                 # optional ‚Äî cross-discussion "see also" links; may be several
  - "<link to a related thought>"
---
## Review
- re [<id>]: **agree** ‚Äî <why / any caveat>      # you accept it; settle may pin this as a decision
- re [<id>]: comment ‚Äî <your take>                # a note / question; keeps the thread open
- re [<id>]: change ‚Äî <what to change & why>      # asks for a revision before you agree
```

- **agree / comment / change** ‚Äî say which, and why. With two people your `agree` settles it; a `change` sends it back; a `comment` with a real question keeps it open.
- **An alignment thought** (a framing) gets the same: `agree` = "I'm on board with this framing", `comment` = a take, `change` = "reframe X".
- **A review can be a closer ‚Äî say so.** An `agree`, or a `comment` that only *informs* (an execution note, a heads-up), is **self-closing**: the counterpart owes you nothing back. Make it unmistakable in-line ‚Äî *"FYI, no reply needed unless you disagree"* ‚Äî so the thread terminates instead of bouncing courtesy acks forever. Only a `change` or a genuine open question keeps it alive. (Termination contract: `relay/AGENTS.md` ‚Üí *Resolution & decisions*.)

### Step 3 ‚Äî Gate + commit
**Show the diff. Wait for OK**, then commit + push.

## Discipline
- **Set `thread` (always) + `re` (the exact thought)** in frontmatter ‚Äî `thread` links the discussion's opening thought (the grouping anchor; `digest` groups by it in O(1), robust to a missing mid-thread `re`); `re` links the immediate thought you answer (omit if it *is* the root). These edges let `digest`/a dashboard **group + compute "settled"** (an answered ask = settled) without parsing prose; an inline `re [<id>]` in the body is *not* enough.
- **Explicit response** ‚Äî `agree` is an affirmative act; silence ‚âÝ agreement.
- **Append-only** ‚Äî write your OWN thought; never edit the one you're answering.
- **Decisions are pinned by settle, not graduated here** ‚Äî an `agree` is what settle harvests; review never writes `decisions/`.
- **Pull before, push after; gate before commit.**

## Anti-patterns (refuse these)
| Temptation | Instead ‚Äî and the tell |
|---|---|
| Infer "they seem fine with it" as agreement | Require an explicit `agree` ‚Äî silence ‚âÝ consent. Tell: about to mark a thread agreed because nobody objected, not because someone said so. |
| Edit the original thought to mark it reviewed | Post your own thought instead ‚Äî append-only, your review is a separate record. Tell: about to open and modify a file you didn't author. |
| Write a `decisions/` file here | Leave that to `relay-settle` ‚Äî decisions are pinned from agreeing reviews, review just responds. Tell: about to write into `decisions/` from inside a review. |

## Companion skills
- **`relay-report`** ‚Äî opens the thoughts you review.
- **`relay-digest`** ‚Äî shows what's `@you` and waiting for your review.
- **`relay-settle`** ‚Äî later pins your agreements as decisions and snapshots the state.

- Use analogies and metaphors frequently to explain complex programming or design concepts (Ë´ãÂ§ö‰ΩøÁî®ÊòìÊáÇÁöÑÊØîÂñª‰æÜËß£ÈáãË§áÈõúÁöÑÁ®ãÂºèÊàñË®≠Ë®àÊ¶ÇÂøµ).

- Keep explanations concise and actionable.
